methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Stratek Global is a growth-stage nuclear energy company founded by Kelvin Kemm. The valuation universe consists of 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private competitors, including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy, providing a sector-level reference set of 11 comparable companies.

The peer-median triangulation method is not applicable in this instance. The nuclear energy sector lacks sufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum density thresholds (0 same-stage peers identified, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier matches). Standard practice requires a minimum of 3 datapoints within filtered peer cohorts to produce reliable median multiples. Broadening to an all-sector median would introduce material methodological bias given the heterogeneity in technology maturity, regulatory status, and capital requirements across the nuclear subsector.

This analysis cannot support a specific valuation range without application of viable triangulation methods. The current dataset establishes the sector composition and identifies named peer institutions, but does not yield quantified multiples, comparable transaction data, or precedent financials that would anchor a defensible estimate. Progression toward a valuation estimate requires either: (1) disclosure of financial metrics and growth rates from Stratek Global itself; (2) public trading or funding data from the identified private peer set; or (3) introduction of alternative methods (discounted cash flow, sum-of-the-parts) with requisite model inputs. Key uncertainties remain the company's revenue stage, path to commercialization, and capital efficiency relative to peers.
